isZip.__returns: Returns true if the given file is a valid ZIP archive, false otherwise.
isZip.__example:
-- extract an entry from a zip archive
local compression = require "compression"

-- check parameters : the archive and entry specified as argument
local archive = arg[1] or error("no archive specified")
local entry = arg[2] or error("no entry specified")

-- check that the file is a valid ZIP archive
if not compression.isZip(archive) then
    error("'"..archive.."' is not a valid ZIP archive, or may be corrupted")
end

-- open the archive
local z = compression.Zip(archive)

-- extract the entry on the disk in the current directory or throws an error if false
local extracted = z:extract(entry) or error(z.error)

print(extracted.name.." has been successfully extracted to "..extracted.path)

__params.__description: A string or a File object, to be checked for ZIP validity.

isZip.__description: The isZip() function checks wether a file represent a valid ZIP archive.

gzip.__returns: Returns a temporary File instance that represent the compressed file.
gzip.__example:
local compression = require "compression"

if #arg == 0 then
    error("error: no input file")
end
-- open a file and compress it to a temporary file
local file = sys.File(arg[1])
local gzipped_file = compression.gzip(file)
-- output the size of the uncompressed and compressed file
print("File size : "..file.size)
print("Gzipped file : "..gzipped_file.size)

-- moves the temporary file to the current directory and rename it
gzipped_file:move(file.name:gsub(file.extension, ".gz"))

__params.__description: A string or a File instance, representing the file to be compressed.

gzip.__description: The gzip() function compress a file using the GZIP file format specification.
gzip.__tinydesc: Compress file using GZIP file format specification

gunzip.__returns: Returns a temporary File instance that represent the decompressed file.
gunzip.__example:
local compression = require "compression"

if #arg == 0 then
    error("error: no input file")
end
-- open the gzip compressed file and decompress it
local file = sys.File(arg[1])
local gunzipped_file = compression.gunzip(file)
-- output the size of the original and decompressed file
print("GZipped file : "..file.size)
print("Decompressed File size : "..gunzipped_file.size)

gunzipped_file:remove()

__params.__description: A string or a File instance, representing the GZIP compressed file.

gunzip.__description: The gunzip() function decompress a GZIP compressed file.
gunzip.__tinydesc: Decompress file using GZIP file format specification

inflate.__returns: Returns a Buffer that holds the compressed data or nil if compression failed.
inflate.__example:
local text = [[Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Sed non risus. Suspendisse lectus 
tortor, dignissim sit amet, adipiscing nec, ultricies sed, dolor. Cras elementum ultrices diam. Maecenas
ligula massa, varius a, semper congue, euismod non, mi. Proin porttitor, orci nec nonummy 
molestie, enim est eleifend mi, non fermentum diam nisl sit amet erat.]]

local compressed = require("compression").deflate(text)

print("size of text in bytes : "..#text)
print("size of compressed buffer in bytes : "..#compressed)
print("compression ratio : "..tostring(math.floor(100-#compressed/#text * 100)).."%")

__params.__description: A string or a Buffer object, to be decompressed.

inflate.__description: The inflate() function decompress data using the INFLATE algorithm.

deflate.__returns: Returns a Buffer that holds the compressed data or nil if compression failed.
deflate.__example:
local text = [[Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Sed non risus. Suspendisse lectus 
tortor, dignissim sit amet, adipiscing nec, ultricies sed, dolor. Cras elementum ultrices diam. Maecenas
ligula massa, varius a, semper congue, euismod non, mi. Proin porttitor, orci nec nonummy 
molestie, enim est eleifend mi, non fermentum diam nisl sit amet erat.]]

local compression = require "compression"
-- Compress text
local compressed = compression.deflate(text)

-- decompress text and check if it is what we started with
print(tostring(compression.inflate(compressed)) == text)

__params.__description: A string or a Buffer object, to be compressed.

deflate.__description: The deflate() function compress data using the DEFLATE algorithm.
deflate.__tinydesc: Compress data using the DEFLATE algorithm

read.__returns:
Returns a Buffer value with the entry content.
Returns false if any error occured. You can check the Zip.error property to get the error message.
read.__example:
-- read an entry in a zip archive and print its content
local compression = require "compression"
local console = require "console"

local archive_name = console.readln("Enter name of the zip archive: ")
local entry_name = console.readln("Enter an entry to print its content: ")
console.writecolor("cyan", entry_name, "from", archive_name, ":\n")

-- open the zip archive in "read" mode
local z = compression.Zip(archive_name)

-- read the entry content or throws an error if false
local content = z:read(entry_name) or error(z.error)

-- prints the content in hexadecimal
console.write(content:encode("hex"))
read.__results: Buffer | boolean

__params.__description: A string representing the path and/or the name of the entry in the Zip archive.

read.__description: Reads an entry in memory at the given path/name in the Zip archive. The Zip archive should have been previously opened in "read" mode.

delete.__returns: Returns the number of deleted entries or false in case of error. You can check the Zip.error property to get the error message.
delete.__example:
-- Remove entries from a zip archive
local compression = require "compression"
local console = require "console"

-- Open the zip archive in "delete" mode
local zipfile = compression.Zip("data.zip", "delete")

-- Deletes two entries
if zipfile:delete("results.xlsx", "doc/readme.txt") then 
  print("Entries have been successfully removed from the data.zip archive")
else 
  print("An error occured during entries deletion : "..zip.error)
end
delete.__description: Deletes one ore more entries from the Zip archive. The Zip archive should have been previously opened in "delete" mode.
delete.__tinydesc: Delete one or more entries from Zip archive

isdirectory.__returns: Returns true if the given entry is a directory, false otherwise.
isdirectory.__example:
-- checks if an entry from a zip archive is a directory
local compression = require "compression"

-- check parameters : the archive and entry specified as argument
local archive = arg[1] or error("no archive specified")
local entry = arg[2] or error("no entry specified")

-- open the archive
local z = compression.Zip(archive)

-- check the entry 
local result = z:isdirectory(entry) and " is " or " is not "

print(entry.." from "..archive..result.."a directory")

print(entry.." from "..archive..result.."a directory")

__params.__description: A string representing the path and/or the name of the entry in the Zip archive.

isdirectory.__description: Check if the entry with the given path/name in the Zip archive represents a directory. The Zip archive should have been previously opened in "read" mode.
isdirectory.__tinydesc: Check for directory entry in Zip archive

extractall.__returns:
Returns the number of extracted entries.
Returns false if any error occured. You can check the zip.error property to get the error message.
extractall.__example:
-- extract an entry from a zip archive
local compression = require "compression"

-- check parameters : the archive and entry specified as argument
local archive = arg[1] or error("no archive specified")

-- open the archive
local z = compression.Zip(archive)

-- extract the archive on the disk in the current directory or throws an error if false
local extracted = z:extract() or error(z.error)

print(extracted.name.." has been successfully extracted to "..extracted.path)
extractall.__description: Extract on the disk all the content of the Zip archive. The Zip archive should have been previously opened in "read" mode.
extractall.__tinydesc: Extract all Zip archive content to disk

reopen.__returns: The function returns no value.

__params.__description:
An optional string that indicate the open mode :

  • "read" : The zip archive is open for reading (the zip archive must exist). It's the default mode.
  • "append" : The zip archive is open for appending (the zip archive must exist).
  • "write" : The zip archive is open for writing (the zip archive is created and overwrites any previous existing archive).
An optional number that indicate the compression level, from 0 (no compression) to 10 (maximum compression level), defaulting to 6.

reopen.__description: Closes the zip archive and reopens it using the specified mode and level compression.
reopen.__tinydesc: Close and reopen the Zip archive

constructor.__description: The Zip constructor opens the specified ZIP archive using the specified mode. Returns a Zip value representing the ZIP archive.

__params.__description: A string or a File object, representing the ZIP archive to be opened.

An optional string that indicate the open mode :
  • "read" : The zip archive is open for reading (the zip archive must exist). It's the default mode.
  • "append" : The zip archive is open for appending (the zip archive must exist).
  • "write" : The zip archive is open for writing (the zip archive is created and overwrites any previous existing archive).
  • "delete" : The zip archive is open for deleting (Zip.delete() can be used to delete entries).
An optional number that indicate the compression level, from 0 (no compression) to 10 (maximum compression level), defaulting to 6.

iszip64.__description: Checks if the zip archive uses the ZIP64 extensions (for zip archives larger than 4 GiB).
iszip64.__example:
-- inspect a zip archive
local compression = require "compression"
local console = require "console"


-- check parameters : the archive specified as argument
local archive = arg[1] or error("no archive specified")

-- open the archive
local z = compression.Zip(archive)

console.writeln("Inspecting "..archive.."...")
console.writecolor("lightgreen", "Total size:\t")
console.writeln(z.size.." bytes")
console.writecolor("lightgreen", "Total entries:\t")
console.writeln(z.count)
console.writecolor("lightgreen", "ZIP64:\t\t")
console.writeln(z.iszip64 and "enabled" or "disabled")
iszip64.__tinydesc: Check if the Zip archive uses ZIP64 extensions

Zip.__tinydesc: Object representation of a ZIP archive file

error.__description: Get the last error message after a zip operation failed.
error.__example:
-- always prints an error message (entry does not exists)
local compression = require "compression"

-- check parameters : the archive specified as argument
local archive = arg[1] or error("no archive specified")

-- open the archive
local z = compression.Zip(archive)

-- always throws an error using the z.error message property
local result = z:extract("dummy entry") or error(z.error)
error.__tinydesc: Get last error message after failed Zip operation

write.__returns: Returns true if entry has been successfully added to the zip archive or false otherwise. You can check the Zip.error property to get the error message.
write.__example:
-- zip script
local compression = require "compression"

-- check if the file specified as argument exists
local f = sys.File(arg[1] or error("no file specified"))

if f.exists then
	--- create a zip archive file with the same name
	local archive = compression.Zip(f.name:gsub(f.extension, ".zip"), "write")
	--- add the file to the archive
	archive:write(f)
else
	error(f.name.." not found")
end

__params.__description: An optional string representing the path and/or the name of the new entry in the Zip archive. If omitted, the new entry will be archived at the root of the Zip archive, using the name provided by the entry parameter.

write.__description: Writes a new entry in the Zip archive. The Zip archive should have been previously opened in "write" or "append" mode.

close.__returns: The function returns no value.
close.__description:
Closes the zip archive.
Zip values that are garbage collected (for example when going out of scope) are automatically closed before destruction. In this case, it's not necessary to call the Zip:close() method.

Zip length operator.__description: The length operator # applied on a Zip object returns the total number of entries that the Zip archive contains. The Zip.count property returns the same value.
Zip length operator.__example:
local compression = require "compression"

-- check parameters : the archive specified as argument
local archive = arg[1] or error("no archive specified")

-- open the archive
local z = compression.Zip(archive)

-- #z is equivalent to z.count
print(archive.." has a total of "..#z.." entries")
Zip length operator.__tinydesc: Get the number of entries in the Zip archive

Zip archive iteration.__description:
Zip objects are iterable through the Luart helper function each(), returning two values at each iteration :

  • An entry name as a string.
  • A boolean value indicating wether the current entry is a Directory or not.
Zip archive iteration.__example:
-- lists all entries in specified archive
local compression = require "compression"

-- check parameters : the archive specified as argument
local archive = arg[1] or error("no archive specified")

-- open the archive
local z = compression.Zip(archive)

-- #z is equivalent to z.count
print(archive.." has a total of "..#z.." entries")
Zip archive iteration.__tinydesc: Iterate over the Zip archive, entry by entry

async.extractall.__returns:
This function returns a Task executed in background.
Once the Task has terminated the entry extraction, it will return the number of extracted entries or false if any error occured. You can check the zip.error property to get the error message.
async.extractall.__example:
-- extract an entry from a zip archive
local compression = require "compression"

-- check parameters : the archive and entry specified as argument
local archive = arg[1] or error("no archive specified")

-- open the archive
local z = compression.Zip(archive)

-- extract all the archive on the disk in the current directory, asynchronously or throws an error if false
local extracted = await(z:extractall() or error(z.error))

print(extracted.name.." has been successfully extracted to "..extracted.path)
async.extractall.__description: Extract on the disk all the content of the Zip archive, asynchronously. The Zip archive should have been previously opened in "read" mode.

extract.__returns:
Returns a File or a Directory representing the extracted entry on the disk.
Returns false if any error occured. You can check the Zip.error property to get the error message.
extract.__example:
-- extract an entry from a zip archive
local compression = require "compression"

-- check parameters : the archive and entry specified as argument
local archive = arg[1] or error("no archive specified")
local entry = arg[2] or error("no entry specified")

-- open the archive
local z = compression.Zip(archive)

-- extract the entry on the disk in the current directory or throws an error if false
local extracted = z:extract(entry) or error(z.error)

print(extracted.name.." has been successfully extracted to "..extracted.path)
extract.__results: File | Directory | boolean

__params.__description: A string representing the path and/or the name of the entry in the Zip archive to be extracted.
An optional boolean value that indicates to extract the entry conserving its path or not.

extract.__description: Extract on the disk an entry with the given path/name in the Zip archive. The Zip archive should have been previously opened in "read" mode.
extract.__tinydesc: Extract an entry from Zip archive

async.extract.__returns:
This function returns a Task executed in background, or false in case of error.
Once the Task has terminated the entry extraction, it will return one of the following possible values :
  • File representing the extracted file on the disk.
  • Directory representing the extracted directory on the disk.
  • false if any error occured. You can check the Zip.error property to get the error message.
async.extract.__example:
-- extract an entry from a zip archive
local compression = require "compression"

-- check parameters : the archive and entry specified as argument
local archive = arg[1] or error("no archive specified")
local entry = arg[2] or error("no entry specified")

-- open the archive
local z = compression.Zip(archive)

-- extract the entry on the disk asynchronously or throws an error if false
local extracted = await(z:extract(entry) or error(z.error))

print(extracted.name.." has been successfully extracted to "..extracted.path)

__params.__description: A string representing the path and/or the name of the entry in the Zip archive to be extracted.
An optional boolean value that indicates to extract the entry conserving its path or not.

async.extract.__description: Extract on the disk an entry with the given path/name in the Zip archive, asynchronously. The Zip archive should have been previously opened in "read" mode.

size.__description: Get the size in bytes of the zip archive.
size.__example:
-- prints the size on the disk of a zip archive
local compression = require "compression"

-- check parameters : the archive and entry specified as argument
local archive = arg[1] or error("no archive specified")

-- open the archive
local z = compression.Zip(archive)

-- prints the size of the zip archive in bytes
print(archive.." has a size of "..z.size)
size.__tinydesc: Get total size in bytes of the Zip archive

file.__description: Get the File object representing the current opened ZIP archive.
file.__example:
-- prints the total number of entries from a zip archive
local compresion = require "compression"

-- check parameters : the archive specified as argument
local archive = arg[1] or error("no archive specified")

-- open the archive
local z = compression.Zip(archive)

-- prints ZIP archive properties
print("Archive fullpath\t: "..z.file.fullpath)
print("Archive size\t: "..z.file.size)

Zip.__description: The Zip object creates, reads, and extracts ZIP archive files

count.__description: Get the number of entries in the zip archive.
count.__example:
-- prints the total number of entries from a zip archive
local compression = require "compression"

-- check parameters : the archive specified as argument
local archive = arg[1] or error("no archive specified")

-- open the archive
local z = compression.Zip(archive)

-- z.count property is equivalent to #z
print(archive.." has a total of "..z.count.." entries")

__description: The compression module allows for use and modification of ZIP and GZIP files
